title: practical english usage - Part 284
content:
A  machine that dries things is a dryer.

Two   spellings are possible for the nouns flyer/flier.

story -> stories spy -> spies hurry -> hurries forms in -ies.

Generally, nouns and verbs that end in -y have plural or third person singular happy -> happily marry -> marriage  easy easier   busy -> business hurry -> hurried   fury -> furious  merry -> merriment When we  add an ending to a word that ends in -y, we  usually change -y to -/-.

hopping /'hopɪŋ/                  latter /'lætə(r)/                     dinner /'dmə(r)/ hoping /'həopɪŋ/                    later /'leɪ t ə(r)/                     diner /'dainə(r)/ usually pronounced as a long vowel or as a diphthong (double vowel). Compare: because, in the middle of a word, a stressed vowel letter before one consonant is The  reason for doubling is to show that a vowel is still pronounced short. This is 9 Why   double?

Word  Formation and SpellingSection 30 vocabulary' •  350  ie and *1 throu(gh) ti(gh)t wei(gh) mi(gh)t nei(gh)bour ni(gh)t ou(gh)t ri(gh)t strai(gh)t thou(gh)t •  borou(gh) bou(gh)t eau(gh)t dau(gh)ter hei(gh)t hi(gh) li(gh)t •  ehampa(g)ne forei(g)n si(g)n •  han(d)kerehief san(d)wieh We(d)nesday •  mus(e)le •  elim(b) eom(b) de(b)t dou(b)t dum(b) The  letters in brackets are not pronounced.

2  usually      three     syllables,      not   four (AmE three syllables) diff(e)rent        med( i)eine ehoe(o)late       marri(a)ge        sev(e)ral bus(i)ness        ev(e)ning         rest(au)rant asp(i)rin         ev(e)ry          om(e)lette The  letters in brackets are usually not pronounced.

1  usually      two    syllables,      not   three Here is a list of some difficult common words with their pronunciations.

hundred years, while our spelling system has stayed more or less the same.

mainly because our pronunciation has changed a good deal over the last few in many English words, the spelling is different from the pronunciation. This is 351  spelling      and   pronunciation For other pronunciations of either and neither, ►     156-157.

Exceptions: seize, Neil, Keith, either, neither ceiling deceive receive receipt believe chief field grief piece shield 'i before e, except after e'.

we  write ei after c for this sound. English-speaking children learn a rhyme: The  sound /i:/ (as in believe) is often written ie, but not usually ei. However, 350 /e  and   e/ break  book  week  peach  coach bank  work  talk   march  bench After a consonant or two vowels, we  write -k and -ch.
